summary: Dual-use Chromium credential extraction tool with unreferenced obfuscated bytenode bundle
details: Package advertises and implements local extraction of Chromium-based browser (Chrome, Edge, Brave) cookies and saved passwords on Windows, including an App-Bound Encryption (v20) bypass: addon.cpp spawns the target browser via CreateProcessW with DEBUG_PROCESS, sets a hardware execute breakpoint at OSCrypt.AppBoundProvider.Decrypt.ResultCode, and uses ReadProcessMemory to capture the 32-byte AES key, then reads Local State and calls CryptUnprotectData via ExtractMasterKey. Extracted credentials are written to local files for the operator; no network exfiltration is wired up in index.js, cli.js, src/addon.cpp, or binding.gyp, and the tool is user-invoked through the 'baksen' CLI rather than auto-running on install (the install script only triggers node-gyp rebuild). Two additional concerns: (1) the declared main entry require('./index.jsc') is not shipped, so default require() of the package fails — the working surface is the CLI only; (2) src/license.jsc is a bytenode-compiled, heavily obfuscated ~40KB bundle present in the tarball but not reached from any traced entry point. The obfuscated bundle is not currently executed, but its presence alongside offensive credential-extraction primitives warrants human review before this package is trusted in a feed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
